articles.headline: Five aviation rivalries that define competition in the modern era

articles.headline: Starcloud achieves unicorn status with $170 million raise for orbital data centers
articles.blurb: Starcloud has raised $170 million to accelerate development of its next-generation spacecraft, reaching a $1.1 billion valuation as it awaits permission to deploy an 88,000-strong orbital data center network.
articles.takeaway: Orbital data centers could reshape aerospace infrastructure economics by offering latency-free computing directly from space, creating new commercial opportunities beyond traditional satellite services.

articles.headline: Best Transpacific Airlines (Premium Comfort Revealed)

articles.takeaway: Premium cabin differentiation on transpacific routes has become a critical revenue driver as ultra-long-haul competition intensifies, with comfort amenities now directly influencing route profitability and market share.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern SMS frameworks shift accountability from post-accident investigation to continuous preventive risk identification, fundamentally changing how airlines allocate safety resources.

articles.takeaway: Artemis 2 integrates scientific research objectives into crew operations, establishing precedent for balancing engineering validation with productive payload missions on deep-space exploration flights.

articles.headline: SMS Declaration of Compliance
articles.blurb: What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.
articles.takeaway: Operators must formally document their safety management system maturity and compliance gaps to the FAA, creating accountability beyond mere policy existence.

articles.headline: Why Do Airlines Still Invest In Premium Economy Instead Of Business Class?

articles.takeaway: Airlines are prioritizing premium economy's superior revenue-per-square-foot efficiency over business class expansion to optimize cabin density and profitability.

articles.blurb: An easyJet cabin crew member required emergency medical assistance.

articles.headline: Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

articles.takeaway: Wire strikes kill helicopters faster than pilot reflexes can respond, making pre-flight route planning and terrain awareness the only viable mitigation strategies.

articles.headline: 7 Reasons Why The F-22 Still Outperforms Newer Jets
articles.blurb: While the F-35 now outperforms F-22 as a balanced advanced fighter, the F-22 still wins in its air dominance role and some flying characteristics.
articles.takeaway: The F-22's specialized air dominance design continues outperforming newer multi-role fighters in specific mission profiles, highlighting the enduring value of role-optimized aircraft architecture.

articles.headline: Why Do Cargo Airlines Still Prefer The Boeing 747 Over Newer Jets?
articles.blurb: The new generation of jets has pushed out the icons of old, but one is still hanging around and is actually preferred over many of these new options.
articles.takeaway: The 747's unique upper-deck cargo capacity and established ground infrastructure create economic advantages that newer aircraft designs haven't fully replicated for freight operators.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-airspace systems offers immediate efficiency gains while capacity-expanding technologies remain under development for long-term sustainability.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: What Happens To Aircraft After Retirement?
articles.blurb: The fate of retired commercial jets is a mystery to many, but in reality, there are many interesting journeys that they can go on.
articles.takeaway: Retired aircraft generate substantial secondary market value through conversion, leasing, and parts harvesting, extending their economic utility beyond their primary service lives.

articles.headline: The Striking Differences Between The Airbus A321XLR & Boeing 737 MAX 10
articles.blurb: The Airbus A321XLR and Boeing 737 MAX 10 represent the pinnacle of evolution in the aviation industry, pushing narrowbodies to their absolute limits.
articles.takeaway: The A321XLR's 4,700+ nautical mile range fundamentally reshapes route economics by enabling transatlantic service without widebody costs, while the 737 MAX 10's larger cabin maximizes high-density domestic profitability.

articles.headline: Press Release: Aer Lingus takes off with Starlink inflight Wi-Fi

articles.headline: ‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings
articles.blurb: While NASA outlined plans to increase the cadence of robotic lunar lander missions at a recent event, it said little about accelerating work on crewed lunar landers.
